title: centaurea pterocaula subsp. iranica
intro: Centaurea pterocaula subsp. iranica, also known as Iranian knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its rich content of essential oils and bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Centaurea pterocaula subsp. iranica have been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications to take advantage of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
